Title: Explainable AI Approach for Classifying Fruit and Vegetable Health Condition Status Using CNN and Grad-CAM
Source title: 7th IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Engineering and Technology, IICAIET 2025
Affiliations: Al-Nahrain University, College of Science, Computer Science Department, Baghdad, Iraq; Queensland University of Technolgy, School of Electrical Engineering and Robotics, Brisbane, Australia
Authors with affiliations: Yousif R., Al-Nahrain University, College of Science, Computer Science Department, Baghdad, Iraq; Al-Dulaimi K., Al-Nahrain University, College of Science, Computer Science Department, Baghdad, Iraq, Queensland University of Technolgy, School of Electrical Engineering and Robotics, Brisbane, Australia
Abstract: Accurate classification and identification of healthy via rotten fruits are vital for ensuring the quality of agricultural products and optimizing food supply chains. However, traditional methods are generally manual, time-consuming, and prone to inconsistency. A key contribution in the proposed model is the integration of (Grad-CAM), which can offer visual explanations of the CNN's decision-making process for the automatic classification of fruits conditions, specifically recognizing between healthy or diseased. This proposed method improves model interpretability and also contributes to more effective training. These findings have the potential of the system as a robust tool for real-time sorting and quality control in agricultural environments. Experimental results have demonstrated high classification performance, with accuracy 95% in certain scenarios.  © 2025 IEEE.

Authors: Suman Y.R.; Varun M.; Sathiya Prakash G.; Nithin H.; Deepak N.R.; Omprakash B.
Title: Enhancing Crop Disease Prediction: A Hybrid Approach Integrating Deep Learning, Ensemble Methods, and Explainable AI
Source title: Proceedings of 3rd IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems, ICKECS 2025
Affiliations: Atria It, Vtu, Dept. of Information Science & Engineering, Karnataka, Bangalore, India
Authors with affiliations: Suman Y.R., Atria It, Vtu, Dept. of Information Science & Engineering, Karnataka, Bangalore, India; Varun M., Atria It, Vtu, Dept. of Information Science & Engineering, Karnataka, Bangalore, India; Sathiya Prakash G., Atria It, Vtu, Dept. of Information Science & Engineering, Karnataka, Bangalore, India; Nithin H., Atria It, Vtu, Dept. of Information Science & Engineering, Karnataka, Bangalore, India; Deepak N.R., Atria It, Vtu, Dept. of Information Science & Engineering, Karnataka, Bangalore, India; Omprakash B., Atria It, Vtu, Dept. of Information Science & Engineering, Karnataka, Bangalore, India
Abstract: This review examines how hybrid models, grouped learning, and explain/reason-able AI can enhance the sensing and detection of crop diseases, which is vital for increasing farm productivity and ensuring global food security. A novel approach combining ResNet50 and VGG16 provides high accuracy in detecting diseases in crops such as cashew, tomato, cassava, and maize. By employing grouping techniques with tools like CNN, ResNeXt, and InceptionV3, the system turned out remarkably more effective by combining their individual strengths. To build trust, the research also incorporates explainable AI methods like S.H.A.P and L.I.M.E, which help farmers and experts understand how the AI arrives at its predictions. Incorporating weather data, such as temperature and humidity, further enhances the system's accuracy, as these factors play a vital role in how diseases spread. This approach not only delivers accurate results but also emphasizes the importance of transparency in AI tools. By combining advanced technology with clear explanations, this study showcases how AI can help manage crop diseases sustainably, supporting farmers to ensure a stable food supply for the future. This unique method has the potential to change farming practices, reduce crop losses, and contribute to global food security.  © 2025 IEEE.

Authors: Rasheed S.; Mirza A.; Saeed M.S.; Yousaf M.H.
Author full names: Rasheed, Shafaq (57222113211); Mirza, Amal (59700077500); Saeed, Muhammad Saad (57224071221); Yousaf, Muhammad Haroon (15078604400)
Title: Trash Detection in Water Bodies Using Yolo With Explainable AI Insight
Source title: 6th International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry, ICRAI 2024
Affiliations: University of Engineering and Technology, Department of Computer Engineering, Taxila, Pakistan; National Center of Robotics and Automation, Swarm Robotics Lab, Taxila, Pakistan
Authors with affiliations: Rasheed S., University of Engineering and Technology, Department of Computer Engineering, Taxila, Pakistan; Mirza A., National Center of Robotics and Automation, Swarm Robotics Lab, Taxila, Pakistan; Saeed M.S., National Center of Robotics and Automation, Swarm Robotics Lab, Taxila, Pakistan; Yousaf M.H., University of Engineering and Technology, Department of Computer Engineering, Taxila, Pakistan
Abstract: Waste disposal into water bodies is a serious concern for environmental engineers, often resulting in urban flooding, soil degradation in agricultural areas, and freshwater pollution. Additionally, trash accumulation in water bodies threatens ma-rine life by introducing contaminants through storm drains, rivers, and other channels. Furthermore, the unique character-istics of underwater environments, including high noise levels, low visibility, and color variation, pose challenges for trash detection. Thus, addressing this issue is crucial for environmental sustainability. The work focuses on both floating and submerged trash in water bodies. Typically, the detection of trash on water bodies is impacted by environmental variability, diverse appearance, and background clutter. These conditions also make usual object detection techniques less effective. Deep learning methods have shown promise in handling challenging visual tasks, therefore researchers have turned to them to get over these challenges. In this work, we focus on applying different versions of YOLO to the problem of water trash detection. To assess these models' performance, we use four different datasets: Trash Flow rate Dataset, DeepTrash, FloW, and TrashICRA. Each dataset represents various trash types under different conditions such as underwater trash and floating trash. Our study attempts to identify the best method suitable for the detection of aquatic waste. Our results highlight the effectiveness of YOLO variants in identifying the presence of trash in water ecosystems. By leveraging Grad-CAM we gain insights into the interpretability and transparency of deep learning models thus enabling Explainable AI. This approach will also contribute to the understanding and trustworthiness of artificial systems deployed for trash detection applications by providing a transparent view of decision making process of deep learning models. © 2024 IEEE.

Title: Explainable AI Meets MobileNetV2: A Multi-Branched Approach for Apple Leaf Disease Identification
Source title: Applied Fruit Science
Affiliations: Department of Computer Science & Technology, Faculty of Engineering, Central University of Punjab, Punjab, Bathinda, India
Authors with affiliations: Jalal M., Department of Computer Science & Technology, Faculty of Engineering, Central University of Punjab, Punjab, Bathinda, India; Farooq U., Department of Computer Science & Technology, Faculty of Engineering, Central University of Punjab, Punjab, Bathinda, India; Kaur A., Department of Computer Science & Technology, Faculty of Engineering, Central University of Punjab, Punjab, Bathinda, India
Abstract: Apple farming plays a significant role in agriculture, serving as an essential source of livelihood for farmers. However, cedar apple rust, apple scab, and black rot are common apple leaf diseases severely affecting apple yield. Early detection of these diseases is crucial for preserving quality and productivity. Researchers have used deep learning models to improve disease classification, they but lack lightweight architecture and transparency, operating as black box systems. Leveraging the power of lightweight models and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) addresses these challenges by developing transparent methods based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). This study proposes an enhanced version of MobileNetV2, incorporating a multi-branched architecture to improve feature map representation for classification tasks. The proposed model achieved 99.18% accuracy on the benchmark plant village dataset, surpassing existing studies. The results also integrated local interpretable model-agnostic explanations (LIME) to emphasize the role of individual features in the model’s predictions. The proposed model has a lightweight structure, which ensures its suitability for IoT-based real-time agricultural applications. © The author(s), exclusively licensed to Springer-Verlag GmbH Germany, a part of Springer Nature 2025.

Authors: Vasantha B.; Dhumpati R.
Author full names: Vasantha, B. (60503870300); Dhumpati, Raghu (59252025200)
Title: A Literature Survey on Deep Learning and Explainable AI Approaches for Crop Disease Prediction
Source title: Proceedings of International Conference on Sustainable Communication Networks and Application, ICSCN 2025
Affiliations: Kakatiya University, CSE, India; Bahrain Polytechnic, Department of CSE, Bahrain
Authors with affiliations: Vasantha B., Kakatiya University, CSE, India; Dhumpati R., Bahrain Polytechnic, Department of CSE, Bahrain
Abstract: The agricultural sector faces significant challenges in sustaining crop yield due to the increasing prevalence of plant diseases. Recent advancements in deep learning (DL) have introduced scalable and accurate methods for image-based crop disease diagnosis. However, despite notable progress, limitations persist in model generalization, interpretability, and comparative assessment with classical approaches. This survey critically examines state-of-the-art DL frameworks, including EfficientNet variants, attention mechanisms like Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM), and advanced augmentation strategies such as CutMix and CLAHE. We explore the integration of Explainable AI (XAI) techniques - Grad-CAM++, SHAP, and LIME - into plant disease detection models to enhance interpretability for agronomists. A taxonomy of datasets, including GPID-22, PlantSeg, and real-world noisy benchmarks like the Rice Leaf Disease dataset, is presented. Furthermore, we compare the performance and robustness of DL architectures with traditional machine learning classifiers such as SVM, KNN, and Random Forest using hybrid feature embeddings. The survey highlights the critical need for explainability, benchmarking, and uncertainty quantification in agricultural AI applications. By consolidating recent findings and identifying persistent research gaps, this work aims to guide future developments toward trust-worthy, transparent, and field-deployable crop disease prediction systems.  © 2025 IEEE.

Author full names: Radhakrishnan, Menaka (59958410400); Monish, Neerajaksha (60002781500); Dev, Parimi Siva (59958410500); Kesavan, Neha (59957980100); Thomas, Nora Sara (59957980200)
Title: Implementation of Explainable AI in Deep Learning Methods for Multiclass Classification of Plant Diseases in Mango Leaves
Source title: Electronic Letters on Computer Vision and Image Analysis
Affiliations: Centre for Cyber Physical Systems, Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, India; School of Electronics Engineering, Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, India
Authors with affiliations: Radhakrishnan M., Centre for Cyber Physical Systems, Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, India; Monish N., School of Electronics Engineering, Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, India; Dev P.S., School of Electronics Engineering, Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, India; Kesavan N.; Thomas N.S., School of Electronics Engineering, Vellore Institute of Technology, Chennai, India
Abstract: Maintaining optimal yield plays a crucial role in the prosperity of agriculture and in turn the economy of the country. One way to optimize this yield is by early and accurate detection and diagnosis of crop diseases. Traditional methods that involve manual inspection or the like tend to be tedious and often inaccurate. Hence, the use of machine learning and convolutional neural networks have proven to be of great advantage in terms of accuracy, reliability, ease of implementation etc. This paper explores various deep learning models such as AlexNet, ResNet, Swin Transformer, Vgg-16, vit model for plant leaf disease detection and classification on a dataset of mango leaves and compares aspects such as accuracy and loss. Further the models have been combined using feature fusion, and their accuracies were compared. Finally, a combination of ResNet and AlexNet has been proposed with an impressive accuracy of 99.97%. Further, Grad-CAM (Gradient weighted Class Activation Mapping) has been implemented to highlight important regions in the leaf images which improves visualization. This can potentially provide an accurate identification and classification of plant diseases based on leaf images. © (2024), (Universitat Autonoma de Barcelona). All Rights Reserved.
